description: Create a dedicated ticket activity/audit table migration with tenant, ticket, event, actor, source, entity, changes, details, occurred_at, and created_at fields.
Add tenant/ticket/time indexes that support efficient timeline reads for a single ticket.
Define TypeScript types/constants for ticket activity event types, actor types, entity types, and sources.
Align ticket activity event names with existing ticket domain events where practical.
Implement a shared activity logging helper that writes with explicit tenant and works in normal and admin transactions.
Ensure activity logging helper fails fast for transactional write failures while tolerating optional display-name enrichment failures.
Implement curated ticket field diffing for status, priority, assignment/team, board, category/subcategory, client/contact, due date, title, response state, and close/reopen fields.
Prevent no-op ticket updates from creating activity entries.
Resolve display labels for curated field diffs where practical while retaining raw IDs in structured changes.
Create activity entries for ticket creation paths used by UI/server actions and shared ticket model flows.
Create activity entries for general curated ticket update paths used by UI/server actions.
Create activity entries for REST API ticket create/update paths in TicketService.
Create activity entries for status changes, including closed and reopened transitions.
Create activity entries for priority changes.
Create activity entries for assignment, unassignment, and team assignment changes.
Create activity entries for board/status move actions.
Create activity entries for response state changes.
Create activity entries for public comment/customer-visible message creation.
Create activity entries for internal note creation.
Create metadata-only activity entries for comment/internal-note edits without storing full old/new body content.
Create activity entries for customer replies from client portal paths.
Create activity entries for inbound email ticket creation with source and safe email metadata.
Create activity entries for inbound email reply/comment creation with source and safe email metadata.
Create activity entries for inbound email reopen transitions.
Create activity entries for bundle/master reopen from child reply behavior.
Create activity entries for ticket document attachment/removal flows where those mutations already exist.
Implement a ticket timeline read model that returns activity entries for a ticket sorted by occurrence time with stable tie-breaking.
Have the timeline read model include or resolve current comment content for comment/note activity entries without duplicating old/new edit bodies.
Enforce existing internal ticket read permissions on the timeline read path.
Do not expose the unified timeline to client portal users in v1.
Add a unified internal activity timeline UI to the ticket detail experience.
Render human-readable timeline entries for ticket creation, field changes, status/close/reopen, priority, assignment, response state, inbound email, and document events.
Render public comments, customer replies, and internal notes chronologically within the unified timeline.
Display actor/source context on timeline entries, including user/contact/system/API/inbound email distinctions.
Handle tickets with no activity rows gracefully without requiring historical backfill.
Ensure timeline UI uses existing Alga UI components and unique IDs for interactive elements.
Document activity event payload conventions and safe metadata rules for future ticket mutation paths.
Avoid storing raw full inbound email bodies or full old/new comment edit bodies in activity rows.
Ensure activity writes in inbound email/admin transaction paths pass explicit tenant and do not rely on `app.current_tenant` GUC.
Keep existing generic audit log helpers untouched except where consolidation is clearly safe and not required for ticket timeline v1.
